title: Citrate-Phosphate Buffer Preparation
author: Neilier Junior
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bfydjps6

steps.instruction: pH range: pH 2.6 to pH 7.0 (a) 0.1 M Citric acid; 19.21 g L -1 (M.W. 192.1 g mol -1 ) (b) 0.2 M Dibasic sodium phosphate; 35.6 g L -1 (dihydrate; M.W. 178.0 g mol -1 ) or 53.6 g L -1 (heptahydrate; M.W. 268.0 g mol -1 ) Mix citric acid and sodium phosphate solutions in the proportions indicated and adjust the final volume to 100 mL with deionized water. %s %s
Adjust the final pH using a sensitive pH meter
